user_message: My Alipay verification is taking too long. What are my options?
assistant_message:
Alipay verification typically takes 24–72 hours. In the meantime:

Primary backup — WeChat Pay accepts the same international cards and verification is often faster.

Secondary backup — UnionPay cards are accepted at most hotels and larger establishments.

Cash — Carry ¥500–800 RMB. International chain hotels accept foreign cards directly.
Open the Emergency toolkit from the chip above for offline numbers. For police dial 110, ambulance 120. Your embassy can assist with lost passports.

user_message: How do I order food without speaking Chinese?
assistant_message: Scan the QR code on your table — most restaurants use mobile ordering. Use picture menus or translation app camera mode. Point at what others are eating!

user_message: How do I use the Beijing subway?
assistant_message: Buy a transit card or use Alipay/WeChat transit QR at station gates. Lines are colour-coded; station names appear in English on newer lines. Avoid rush hour 7:30–9:30 and 17:30–19:30 if possible.

user_message: How do I use Didi as a foreigner?
assistant_message: Download Didi (滴滴出行) and link Alipay or WeChat Pay. Enable English in settings. You can paste your destination from a map app. Show the driver your hotel address in Chinese if needed.

user_message: What etiquette should I know in China?
assistant_message: Greet with a slight nod; handshakes are fine in business. Avoid loud voices in temples and museums. Gift-giving is appreciated but not required. Tipping is not expected except at some high-end hotels.

user_message: Which Great Wall section is best for first-timers?
assistant_message: Mutianyu balances scenery and crowds — cable car available. Badaling is closest to Beijing but busiest. Jinshanling is best for hiking if you have a full day. Book transport the night before.

user_message: I lost my passport. What should I do?
assistant_message: File a police report (110) and get a report number. Contact your embassy immediately for an emergency travel document. Keep copies of your passport and visa separately from the original.
